AMD Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results

SANTA CLARA, Calif. ― November 4, 2025 ― AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) today announced financial results for the third quarter of 2025. Third quarter revenue was a record $9.2 billion, gross margin was 52%, operating income was $1.3 billion, net income was $1.2 billion and diluted earnings per share was $0.75. Data Center Segment 
Data Center Market 
The Data Center segment primarily includes server-class CPUs, GPUs, AI accelerators, DPUs, AI NICs, FPGAs, and adaptive SoC products. We leverage our technology to address the computational, visual data processing and AI workload acceleration needs in the data center market. Modern data centers require high performance, energy efficient, scalable and adaptable compute engines to meet the demand driven by the growing amount of data that needs to be stored, accessed, analyzed and managed. payload.thought: I now have comprehensive coverage across all key dimensions. Let me verify the price drop calculation to understand the market reaction.

I now have all the evidence needed to write a comprehensive memo. Let me summarize what I've gathered:

**Key findings:**
- **Q4 2025 results (filed Feb 3, 2026):** Record revenue $10.3B (+34% YoY), non-GAAP EPS $1.53, non-GAAP gross margin 57%
- **Full year 2025:** Record revenue $34.6B, non-GAAP EPS $4.17
- **Q1 2026 guidance:** Revenue ~$9.8B (±$300M), ~32% YoY growth but ~5% sequential decline; non-GAAP GM ~55%
- **Segments:** Data Center Q4 record $5.4B (+39% YoY); FY2025 DC $16.6B (+32%); Client & Gaming Q4 $3.9B (+37%)
- **MI308 China:** Q4 benefited from $360M reserve release + $390M China revenue; Q1 2026 includes ~$100M MI308 China sales
- **Price action:** Sharp ~22% drop from $246.27 (Feb 2) to $192.50 (Feb 5) post-earnings; currently $207.32, down ~20% from the Jan 23 peak of $259.68
- **Risks:** Export controls on MI308, reliance on third-party manufacturers, intense AI GPU competition (NVIDIA), sequential decline in guidance

args.reason: Sufficient evidence gathered from 6 SEC filings (Q4 2025 8-K, Q3 2025 8-K, FY2025 10-K, Q3 10-Q, and related filings) covering: latest results and guidance, segment drivers (Data Center/Instinct GPU ramp, EPYC, Client), MI308 China export control dynamics, risk factors, and sharp post-earnings price decline (~22% Feb 2-5). Ready to write memo.
